description_long: This is a large, gray military cargo airplane with a high-wing configuration and four turbofan engines mounted under the wings. The fuselage is robust and cylindrical, with a slightly tapered nose and a prominent T-tail at the rear. The wings are straight with a slight dihedral angle and lack visible winglets, while the engines are evenly spaced along the wings. The landing gear is not visible in the images, but the aircraft's size and design suggest a tricycle configuration. The cockpit is positioned at the very front of the fuselage, featuring multiple windows for wide visibility. Markings on the fuselage indicate it is a U.S. Air Force aircraft.
description: A large, gray military cargo airplane with four underwing turbofan engines, a high T-tail, and a forward cockpit. The aircraft features a robust fuselage and high-mounted straight wings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide wingspan, four underwing engines, and the prominent T-tail.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cylindrical fuselage, cockpit windows, and engine placement under the high wings.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, T-tail, and overall planform of the aircraft.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the engine arrangement and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing configuration with straight wings and a slight dihedral angle, no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four turbofan engines mounted under the wings.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear, with two main gear units and one nose gear (not visible but implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
